title: Too-late handover: RLF on target during fast mobility
symptoms: A fast-moving UE repeatedly drops the connection mid-handover and re-establishes on the source cell; radio link failure is declared on the target and T310 expires before access to the target completes.
root_cause: The A3 offset and time-to-trigger are too high for the UE speed, so the measurement report and handover command arrive too late and the serving signal has already faded.
resolution: Lower the A3 offset, shorten time-to-trigger on high-mobility layers, enable speed-based TTT scaling, and consider conditional handover so the UE executes as soon as the entry condition is met.

title: Too-early handover: RLF on the new target just after HO
symptoms: Immediately after a handover the UE suffers radio link failure on the new target cell and re-establishes, often back on the original source cell.
root_cause: Handover triggering is too aggressive toward an unreliable neighbor; the A3 offset is too low and time-to-trigger too short, so the UE hands over to a cell whose signal is only briefly strong.
resolution: Raise the A3 offset and hysteresis and lengthen time-to-trigger; add cell-individual offsets to de-prioritise unstable neighbours.

title: Ping-pong handover between two overlapping cells
symptoms: The UE handovers back and forth between two cells within seconds, generating excessive signalling and brief interruptions.
root_cause: Hysteresis is too small and time-to-trigger too short, so both cells alternately satisfy the A3 entry condition in overlapping coverage.
resolution: Increase hysteresis and time-to-trigger, and tune cell-individual offsets so one cell is clearly preferred in the overlap region.

title: Handover failure on T304 expiry
symptoms: The UE receives the handover command but cannot complete access to the target before T304 expires, declaring handover failure and triggering re-establishment.
root_cause: Target access fails within the T304 window, usually due to a failed random-access attempt, wrong timing advance, target overload, or poor target radio conditions.
resolution: Verify the target cell load and coverage, confirm the contention-free preamble is included in the reconfiguration-with-sync, and check that T304 is long enough for the deployment.

title: Random-access failure to the target during handover
symptoms: During handover the UE gets no random-access response from the target, or repeatedly collides, and the handover stalls.
root_cause: No dedicated contention-free preamble was allocated in the handover command so the UE falls back to contention-based RACH, or the target PRACH configuration and timing advance are wrong.
resolution: Ensure a contention-free RACH resource is signalled in reconfiguration-with-sync, and verify the target PRACH configuration, power ramping, and timing advance.

title: Connected-mode RLF from T310 expiry
symptoms: In connected mode the UE declares radio link failure after a burst of out-of-sync indications; T310 expired without recovery.
root_cause: N310 consecutive out-of-sync indications from lower layers started T310, and T310 expired before N311 in-sync indications arrived, due to a sudden coverage drop or interference spike.
resolution: Investigate coverage and interference at the failure location; tune N310, T310 and N311, and trigger handover earlier via A2/A3 so the UE leaves the failing cell in time.

title: Call drop: no suitable cell before T311 expiry
symptoms: After a radio link failure the UE cannot find a suitable cell and, when T311 expires, drops to idle, appearing as a dropped call.
root_cause: There is a genuine coverage hole; no suitable cell is available for re-establishment within the T311 window.
resolution: Address coverage with tilt, power or a new site; where edge cases are common, review whether T311 allows enough cell-search time before falling back to idle.

title: mmWave beam failure and slow recovery
symptoms: On a millimetre-wave cell the connection stalls or drops when the UE turns or is blocked; logs show beam failure detected and a recovery attempt.
root_cause: The serving beam degraded and the beam-failure-instance counter reached its maximum; beam failure recovery via a candidate-beam RACH succeeded slowly or failed because no good candidate beam was configured.
resolution: Tune beam-failure detection and recovery parameters, widen the candidate SSB/CSI-RS beam set, and verify the beam-failure-recovery RACH resources.

title: Inter-frequency neighbour never reported (no gaps)
symptoms: A strong inter-frequency neighbour is visible on drive tests but the UE never reports it, so no handover occurs and the UE eventually suffers RLF.
root_cause: Measurement gaps are not configured, so the UE cannot measure the other frequency, or the neighbour frequency and cells are missing from the measurement configuration.
resolution: Configure measurement gaps for inter-frequency measurement and add the missing frequency and cells to the measurement object.

title: Network-wide late handovers from a high A3 offset
symptoms: Handovers across the network are consistently late, with measurement reports arriving only when the serving cell is already weak.
root_cause: The A3 offset was raised to suppress interference-driven reports, but the same setting delays legitimate mobility reports.
resolution: Rebalance A3 offset against time-to-trigger, and use event A5 (serving below a threshold and neighbour above a threshold) for coverage-driven handover.

title: Inter-gNB Xn handover preparation failure
symptoms: Inter-gNB handovers over Xn fail during preparation; the source gNB logs a handover-preparation failure and the UE stays on the source or drops.
root_cause: The target gNB rejected the Xn Handover Request through admission control or lack of resources, or the Xn transport link had a problem, and N2-based fallback via the AMF was not available.
resolution: Check target admission and load and the Xn link health, and confirm N2 (NGAP) handover fallback is configured for when Xn is unavailable.

title: Downlink stalls after HO: path switch failure
symptoms: The radio-side handover completes but downlink user data stalls for the UE just after the handover.
root_cause: The path switch from the target gNB to the AMF and UPF failed or was delayed (T420 expiry in newer releases), so downlink packets are still routed toward the source.
resolution: Inspect the NGAP Path Switch Request and Acknowledge exchange and the AMF/UPF signalling, and verify T420 handling.

title: Conditional handover never executes
symptoms: Conditional handover is configured for a fast-mobility route but UEs never execute it, still suffering late-handover RLF.
root_cause: The CHO execution condition (its A3 or A5 threshold) is too strict, or the candidate cell configuration in the conditional reconfiguration is wrong.
resolution: Relax the CHO execution condition and verify the candidate cell and RACH configuration carried in the conditional reconfiguration.

title: Idle-mode cell reselection ping-pong
symptoms: Idle UEs reselect back and forth between two cells, causing extra paging load and battery drain.
root_cause: Qhyst is too low and Treselection too short, so cell ranking oscillates between cells in overlapping coverage.
resolution: Increase Qhyst and Treselection, and apply speed-dependent scaling so fast UEs reselect less aggressively.

title: Secondary cell group failure in NR-DC / EN-DC
symptoms: In NR-DC or EN-DC the secondary cell group drops while the master leg stays up; the UE sends SCG failure information.
root_cause: The SCG hit radio link failure, an SCG random-access problem, or an SCG reconfiguration failure, without tearing down the master cell group.
resolution: Check SCG beam and coverage and the SCG RLF timers; where supported, enable fast MCG recovery so the UE reports the SCG failure over the master leg quickly.

title: Cell-edge failure that is really uplink-limited
symptoms: At the cell edge the UE shows good downlink quality but still fails handover or random access; the problem is on the uplink.
root_cause: The uplink is power-limited: PRACH or PUSCH cannot reach the gNB even though downlink RSRP looks acceptable, so access and handover fail.
resolution: Review uplink power control and link budget, consider a supplementary uplink (SUL) carrier, and set handover thresholds using an uplink-aware metric rather than downlink RSRP alone.

title: Strong carrier invisible to idle UEs (inter-frequency search suppressed)
symptoms: A strong carrier on another NR frequency is visible on drive tests but idle UEs never camp on or reselect to it; UEs stay on the serving cell even when a better layer exists, and load stays skewed to one carrier.
root_cause: SnonIntraSearchP (and/or SnonIntraSearchQ) on the serving cell is set too high, so while the serving cell Srxlev is above SnonIntraSearchP (or Squal above SnonIntraSearchQ) the UE is not required to perform inter-frequency or inter-RAT measurements. The strong neighbour is therefore never measured in idle mode and reselection cannot occur.
resolution: Lower SnonIntraSearchP and SnonIntraSearchQ (or remove them) in SIB2/SIB4 so the UE begins inter-frequency measurements at a higher serving level; confirm the target frequency is broadcast in SIB4 with a valid cellReselectionPriority. Verify with UE logging that inter-frequency measurements start once serving Srxlev drops below the new threshold.

title: Idle UEs never reselect to a higher-capacity NR layer
symptoms: A high-capacity inter-frequency NR layer is deployed and measured by idle UEs, but reselection to it does not trigger; the layer stays under-utilised while the coverage layer is congested.
root_cause: The target inter-frequency carrier is configured with an equal or lower cellReselectionPriority than the serving frequency, and threshX-HighP is set too high, so the high-priority reselection criterion (target Srxlev greater than threshX-HighP for TreselectionNR) is never met.
resolution: Raise the target frequency cellReselectionPriority above the serving frequency, or lower threshX-HighP and threshX-LowP so reselection to the higher-priority layer triggers at a realistic signal level; tune TreselectionNR to avoid oscillation.

title: Strong neighbour measured but fails cell-selection S-criterion
symptoms: Idle UEs measure a strong neighbour cell with good SS-RSRP but never select or reselect to it; the cell behaves as if it were unavailable to camping UEs.
root_cause: Qrxlevmin (and/or Qqualmin) broadcast for the target cell is set too high, so even with strong measured power the cell-selection criterion Srxlev = Qrxlevmeas minus (Qrxlevmin plus Qrxlevminoffset) stays at or below zero and the cell is judged non-suitable.
resolution: Lower Qrxlevmin and Qqualmin in the target cell SIB1 to realistic values for the band and deployment; re-check that Srxlev and Squal become positive for UEs at the cell edge, and remove any unintended qRxLevMinOffset.
